input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (includes android, ios stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK Components

The MAUI workload bundles some Android pieces, but you may need to manage SDK versions explicitly.

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed by the workload
# Typical path: $env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"

# List installed SDK platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Discover available platform versions
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific API level (e.g., 35) and build tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses non-interactively
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
# (type 'y' for each prompt)
```

## 4. Set Up Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK Target (WinUI 3)

```powershell
# The MAUI workload already includes WinUI/Windows App SDK support.
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading):
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 6. Install Visual Studio (Recommended IDE)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then modify the installation to add workloads via CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --passive --norestart
```

> If you prefer VS Code, install the `.NET MAUI` extension instead.

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI 3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Deploy to a running emulator/device
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Discover Correct TFMs Dynamically

If you're unsure which target framework monikers are valid:

```powershell
# See what the template generated in the .csproj
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"

# List installed Android manifest versions
dotnet --info | Select-String -Pattern "android"

# Check available .NET SDK feature bands
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## Key Environment Variables to Set (if needed)

```powershell
# Only if tools can't auto-detect the SDK location:
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk",
  "User"
)
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "JAVA_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\jdk\jdk-17*",
  "User"
)
# Resolve the actual JDK path dynamically:
$jdk = Get-ChildItem "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\jdk" |
  Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $jdk.FullName, "User")
```

---

**Run the steps in order.** The critical dependency chain is: **.NET SDK → MAUI workload → Android SDK/emulator → Developer Mode → create project**. Everything after step 2 for Android vs Windows is independent.
